name: Italy
metaTitle: E-2 Visa for Italian Nationals | Watson Immigration Law
metaDescription: Italy issues over 1,200 E-2 visas annually. Guidance on the E-2 Treaty Investor Visa for Italian nationals — 5-year visa validity, Rome consulate processing, hospitality and fashion investment focus.
intro: Italy is a significant source of E-2 Treaty Investor Visa holders, with over 1,200 visas issued annually. Italian investors are particularly active in hospitality, food and beverage, fashion and luxury goods, design, and professional services — sectors where Italy's world-class reputation creates immediate credibility in the U.S. market. Watson Immigration Law represents Italian entrepreneurs and investors seeking to establish or expand U.S. operations.

sections.heading: Treaty Status & History
sections.body: Italy and the United States have maintained a Treaty of Friendship, Commerce and Navigation since 1961. Italian nationals receive a 5-year visa stamp — one of the longest available in the E-2 program — with 2-year U.S. admission periods per entry.

sections.heading: Common Business Types for Italian E-2 Investors
sections.body: Italian investors in the United States most commonly invest in Italian restaurants and food concepts, wine and spirits importing/distribution, luxury retail and fashion boutiques, design studios, and professional services firms. The strong U.S. appetite for Italian brands and cuisine makes these sectors natural fits for E-2 investment. Italian investors are particularly active in New York, Miami, Chicago, and Los Angeles.

sections.heading: Rome Consulate Processing
sections.body: Italian nationals apply for E-2 at the U.S. Embassy in Rome or the U.S. Consulate General in Naples, Milan, or Florence. The Rome Embassy handles the majority of Italian E-2 applications. Processing times are typically 6–10 weeks after interview scheduling. All supporting documents must be submitted in English or with certified translation.

faqs.question: Do Italian nationals qualify for the E-2 visa?
faqs.answer: Yes. Italy has had an E-2 treaty with the United States since 1961. Italian nationals receive a 5-year visa stamp and are among the top 10 E-2 source countries globally.

faqs.question: Can I open an Italian restaurant in the U.S. on an E-2 visa?
faqs.answer: Yes. Restaurants and food service businesses are among the most common E-2 investments by Italian nationals. The business must meet E-2 substantiality and non-marginality requirements — the restaurant must generate significantly more than the investor's living income and have hiring capacity.

faqs.question: How long does the E-2 visa last for Italian nationals?
faqs.answer: Italian nationals receive a 5-year visa stamp with 2-year U.S. entry admissions, renewable indefinitely.

faqs.question: Can my spouse work in the U.S. under my E-2 visa?
faqs.answer: Yes. E-2 dependent spouses receive automatic U.S. work authorization — no separate EAD application is required.
